title: From Lexicon to AI: A Structured-Data Pipeline for Specialized Conversational Systems in Low-Resource Languages
abstract: arXiv:2606.26112v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Low-resource languages face a critical challenge in AI development: creating specialized conversational systems without access to massive training corpora. We present a systematic methodology for transforming structured linguistic resources into specialized AI systems, demonstrating that expert-curated lexical databases can serve as effective foundations for conversational AI development. Our approach converts Hindi WordNet into 1.25 million diverse instruction-response pairs, fine-tunes a 12B-parameter language model using resource-efficient LoRA with 4-bit quantization. Evaluation through a Hindi language learning chatbot demonstrates that structured-knowledge-based systems achieve superior pedagogical effectiveness (91.0 vs. 79.4-83.6 for general-purpose models) while maintaining competitive semantic performance and exceptional consistency. The complete pipeline demonstrates a proof-of-concept methodology using Hindi for developing

Title: From Lexicon to AI: A Structured-Data Pipeline for Specialized Conversational Systems in Low-Resource Languages
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26112v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Low-resource languages face a critical challenge in AI development: creating specialized conversational systems without access to massive training corpora. translated_title: 从词汇到人工智能：面向低资源语言中专门对话系统的结构化数据流水线
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26112v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：低资源语言在人工智能开发中面临一个关键挑战：在没有大规模训练语料库的情况下创建专门的对话系统。我们提出了一种系统性的方法论，将结构化的语言资源转化为专门的人工智能系统，证明由专家精心策划的词汇数据库可以作为对话式人工智能开发的有效基础。我们的方法将印地语词网转化为125万个多样化的指令-响应对，并使用资源高效的LoRA与4位量化微调了一个120亿参数的语言模型。通过印地语学习聊天机器人的评估表明，基于结构化知识的系统实现了优越的教学效果（91.0，而通用模型为79.4-83.6），同时保持了具有竞争力的语义性能和卓越的一致性。完整的流水线展示了一种使用印地语进行开发的概念验证方法。
tldr: 本文提出了一种系统化方法，利用专家构建的词汇数据库（如印地语词网）生成大规模指令数据，并微调语言模型，从而为低资源语言构建高效且一致的专门对话系统。
en_tdlr: This paper presents a systematic methodology that leverages expert-curated lexical databases (e.g., Hindi WordNet) to generate large-scale instruction data and fine-tune language models, enabling the construction of efficient and consistent specialized conversational systems for low-resource languages.
